runs.notes: The early boss-approach pit-jump start is progress evidence, not validated clearance: combat-v0 moved from the WorldX 2112 low right-jump fall death to a later WorldX 2174 airborne sniper/contact death, with score 4900 and weapon 16, but the run still ended with deaths=1 and bossDefeated=0. The next hypothesis must target the WorldX 2173-2174 high-air contact/sniper transition, not the old W2112 late-pit start.

The W2157 high-air contact patch is rejected as a runtime strategy: it regressed combat-v0 from the prior WorldX 2174 boss-approach death to WorldX 2160 while still ending with deaths=1 and bossDefeated=0. Stop patching this route class by local coordinates; the next attempt needs a new TAS/human state-action fragment or a route-class redesign for the boss-approach high-air enemy cluster.

The bridge low fixed crowd patch is progress evidence, not validated clearance: combat-v0 moved from the WorldX 626 bridge blocker to a later danger-clear death at WorldX 1943 with score 4500 and weapon 16, but the run still ended with deaths=1 and bossDefeated=0. The next hypothesis must target the WorldX 1914-1943 danger-clear falling and low-lane state, not retune the earlier bridge crowd window.

The danger low-lane fall patch is progress evidence, not validated clearance: combat-v0 moved from the WorldX 1943 right+A+B fall death to a later WorldX 2038 danger-clear fixed-threat death with score 4700 and weapon 16, but the run still ended with deaths=1 and bossDefeated=0. The next hypothesis must target the WorldX 2038 fixed-threat cluster and stationary B-only death, not retune the WorldX 1914-1943 low-lane fall window.

The descent-carry patch is progress evidence, not validated clearance: combat-v0 moved from the opening WorldX 286-290 death class to a later bridge-clear death at WorldX 626 with score 1700 and weapon 16, but the run still ended with deaths=1 and no bossDefeated signal. The next hypothesis must target the WorldX 614-626 bridge low fixed-threat crowd window, not retune the earlier WorldX 286 contact frame.

The opening low fixed-threat patch changed the Contra Japan combat matrix blocker from death at WorldX 286 with A+B into a no-death max-frame stall at the same WorldX with down+B. This proves the jump death was suppressed, but the fixed low threat is not cleared and the route is still not validated.

The TAS-style right-down-fire variant avoided the previous max-frame stall but died at WorldX 290 with the same low fixed threat still alive. This rejects direct promotion of right-down-fire for the current single-AI combat route.

The right-only variant still died at WorldX 290. The compact trace shows the route lost horizontal ownership during the preceding descent at WorldX 286, so the next hypothesis must handle the earlier opening descent carry instead of adding another contact-frame low-fixed-threat patch.

The spread turret suppression runtime wiring is progress evidence, not validated clearance: combat-v0 moved from the WorldX 2038 stationary B-only fixed-threat death to a later WorldX 2112 boss-approach-clear death with score 4800 and weapon 16, but the run still ended with deaths=1 and bossDefeated=0. The next hypothesis must target the boss-approach sniper/fall transition around WorldX 2111-2112, not retune the W2038 fixed-threat suppression window.

Contra Japan browser AI botrun did not pass Stage 1. The current survival route dies at WorldX 2068 while holding down-fire against a mid fixed threat, before the boss-approach platform-capture candidate can be reached. Archive this as a failure counterexample and train fixed-threat-route/fixed-threat-hp-lock before revalidating boss approach.

The high fixed-threat station patch changed the J-version run's final input from right+A+B to up+B and raised the score from 2300 to 2400, but the AI still dies at WorldX 2087 with the high forward fixed threat alive. This is progress evidence, not Stage 1 clearance.

The mid fixed-threat recovery patch improved the J-version run from WorldX 2068 to WorldX 2087, but it still dies before Stage 1 clearance. The next counterexample is a high/forward fixed threat while the AI holds right+A+B; this is progress evidence, not a pass.
The combat matrix botrun dies very early at WorldX 286 while attempting A+B under an immediate fixed threat. This is not Stage 1 clearance.
The guard matrix botrun reaches WorldX 2038 but dies under fixed-threat pressure while holding B. This is not Stage 1 clearance.
The loot matrix botrun reaches WorldX 1943 with weapon 16 but dies while preserving a loot/cautious route under fixed-threat pressure. This is not Stage 1 clearance.
The speedrun matrix botrun dies on the first bridge with the route still advancing under immediate threat. This is not Stage 1 clearance.
The survival matrix botrun still dies at WorldX 2087 with default weapon and the high fixed threat alive. This is not Stage 1 clearance.
Current contra-j-good survival-v0 route regression check after switching .env to Japanese ROM. Failed before previous W2087 evidence, max W1722; not promoted.
Validated Contra Japan W241 neutral-fire close-body safety patch from saved state frame 1200; active for 2500 resumed frames, no first-death regression; not a clear.
Contra Japan full-route validation after W241 neutral-fire patch; no death in 7000 frames, max W1465, still not clear and needs efficiency/progression fix.

Contra Japan headless boss-wall regression: directional falling-body split passed unit tests and moved stable failure to W3208/8415; full-clip retreat and W3182 extension were rejected after regressions.

Contra Japan headless boss-wall route-level check: lower contact-duck/right-up station extended survival from 8415 to 8706 but did not damage fixed targets or clear W3208. Treat default-weapon boss-wall threshold work as rejected; next route work must prioritize weapon acquisition before boss wall.

Contra Japan headless weapon-route check: W2260-W2368 late weapon-gate action candidates did not change weapon RAM from 0 and often lost active near W2390. Late rescue is rejected; TAS replay evidence shows valid weapon changes happen earlier at W344 and W1591 in the synchronized 2P TAS environment.
accepted W1471 stall fix; progressed beyond grounded loop.
accepted W2160 high-air carry fix; progressed toward boss approach.
accepted W2438-W2700 14x1 pulse platform chain evidence; progressed to W2838.
accepted W2812 late-edge jump evidence; progressed to W2939.
accepted W2896 boss-entry jump evidence; reached W3208 boss wall with default rifle.
rejected boss late up_fire candidate; delayed death but did not reduce core HP.
rejected boss late neutral_fire candidate; delayed death but did not reduce core HP.
rejected boss late right_fire candidate; did not reduce core HP.
rejected boss late right_up_fire candidate; recovered after loss but no core HP progress.
rejected boss late duck_fire candidate; no core HP progress.
rejected boss late right_duck_fire candidate; no core HP progress.
rejected boss late left_fire candidate; retreated below W3208.
rejected boss late left_jump_fire candidate; retreated below W3208.
rejected boss late jump_right_fire candidate; no core HP progress.

Rejected bounded boss-wall candidate 8/12: no Stage 1 clear, no boss-wall clear, no no-death progress beyond W3208; best failure held until lostFrame 8597 after reducing side fixed target pressure, then died at W3204 low-lane contact with core HP still 31.

Zero-death Stage 1 boss-wall batch: 12 segment-first candidates/comparisons tested from W3120/W3208; no candidate exceeded W3208 or cleared boss wall. Representative rejected evidence is phase-enabled W3208 rerun; see docs for candidate list.
Accepted segment-first weapon route evidence: from saved W674 weapon16 state, left_jump_fire escaped the body contact and reached no-death W1210 with weapon16 preserved; not a Stage 1 clear.

Rejected connector with useful best state: W1160-W1235 jump_right_fire carried weapon16 no-death to W1461, but later lost active at frame 4931; save state jp-stage1-w1461-weapon16-20260612 is the next segment-first starting point.

Rejected weapon16 danger-window connector: chained from saved W1715 weapon16 state, route-plan reached no-death W1753 with weapon16 preserved, then lost active at frame 5604. Runtime state jp-stage1-w1753-weapon16-20260612 is retained as the next segment-first analysis point; not a validated clear fragment.

Rejected weapon16 W2309/W2390 connector: W1753 route chain preserved weapon16 and advanced no-death progress to W2390, then lost active at frame 6260 from a low pit/edge fall near W2391. Runtime state jp-stage1-w2390-weapon16-20260612 is retained for analysis; not a validated clear fragment.

Rejected weapon16 boss-approach connector: split-edge W2334-W2352 release and W2688-W2735 jump-right-fire advanced the no-death weapon16 branch beyond W2390; W2735-W2790 right-fire landing fix reached no-death W2838 with weapon16, then lost active at W2839 from an unrecoverable low fall. Runtime state jp-stage1-w2838-weapon16-20260612 is retained; not a validated clear fragment.

Rejected weapon16 boss-approach connector with measurable progress: W2790-W2804 right-fire release plus W2804-W2884 pulse-jump-right-fire advanced the no-death weapon16 branch from W2838 to W2939, then lost active at W2940/Y236 from a low-fall window before the boss wall. Runtime state jp-stage1-w2939-weapon16-20260612 is retained; not a validated clear fragment.

Rejected W2939 follow-up sweep: four one-frame local rescue actions from jp-stage1-w2939-weapon16-20260612 and eight W2884-W2964 descent variants all failed to exceed no-death W2939; representative right-fire extension still lost active at W2940/Y236. Treat W2939 saved state as inspection-only and rebuild the height curve before W2884.

Accepted as incremental zero-death progress only: W2804-W2884 p8/w1 pulse advanced the weapon16 branch from W2939 to reproducible no-death W2941, then lost at W2942/Y236. Follow-up W2884-W2964 post-control sweep did not exceed W2941; not a boss-wall clear or Stage 1 clear.

Accepted as incremental zero-death progress only: W2735-W2784 right_fire followed by W2784-W2884 p12/w1 pulse advanced the weapon16 branch from reproducible W2941 to reproducible no-death W2945, then lost at W2946/Y236 with weapon=16. The prearc batch rejected earlier p8 starts, delayed p8 starts, W2688 jump-release variants, and p10/p8w2 alternatives; not a boss-wall clear or Stage 1 clear.

Rejected W2945 p12 neighbor and post-control sweep: 12 pulse-neighbor variants around W2784-W2884 and 12 W2910-W2964 post-control variants failed to exceed no-death W2945. Representative post-control right_fire still lost at W2946/Y236 with weapon=16. Treat W2945 as an earlier trajectory problem, not a W2910+ local rescue point.

Rejected W2688-W2784 entry-shape sweep: W2688 jump-end variations tied W2945, while W2735-W2784 second-jump or early-pulse variants regressed to W2828-W2857 low-fall deaths. Representative W2688-W2730 jump then right_fire to W2784 still lost at W2946/Y236 with weapon=16. Treat the p12 route as sensitive to premature second-jump release; next search should alter the W2804-W2884 height curve or earlier platform capture without W2735-W2784 second jumps.

Rejected W2860-W2905 grounded late-jump sweep from the W1753 weapon16 continuation: 12 candidates tested around the W2869 grounded frame and W2784-W2884 p12 arc. No candidate exceeded the existing W2945 zero-death ceiling or reached W2960. Forcing jump too early at W2864/W2868 regressed to W2937-W2941; W2872/W2876 forced jump and late p6/p10 pulses only tied W2945; skipping the W2861 pulse regressed to W2942. Treat W2860-W2905 late grounded jump as exhausted for this route class; next search should move earlier than W2784 or use a different platform capture route.

Rejected W2644-W2784 early-capture sweep from the W1753 weapon16 continuation: 12 candidates tested around pre-capture control and W2680-W2688 platform-jump timing. No candidate exceeded the existing W2945 zero-death ceiling or reached W2960. W2686 jump start tied W2945; W2680 starts regressed to W2838; W2682/W2684 starts died around W2730; forced W2644-W2688 right/right-up/right-duck pre-capture produced only post-death W3208 traces with no-death capped at W2724. Treat early jump before W2686 and forced pre-capture right movement as exhausted for this route class.

Rejected W2945 last-frame escape sweep from saved state jp-stage1-w2945-weapon16-20260612: 12 direct pre-death controls were tested at W2945/Y230 with weapon16, including left/left-up/left-jump/left-duck/neutral/up/duck/right-duck/right-up/right/jump-right fire variants. Every candidate lost active on the next frame around W2944-W2946/Y236 and none exceeded the existing W2945 zero-death ceiling or reached W2960. Treat the W2945 pre-death frame as already unrecoverable for single-frame direction/fire/jump control; future work must change the arc before W2945.

Rejected W2440-W2644 pre-capture shape sweep from the W1753 weapon16 continuation: 12 candidates modified the segment before the W2688 platform capture while returning to the same W2735-W2784 and W2784-W2884 p12 baseline. No candidate exceeded the existing W2945 zero-death ceiling or reached W2960. Forced jumps from W2440/W2480/W2520/W2560/W2600 and delayed pulse families mostly died around W2496/Y236; p18/p20 pulse shaping reached only W2594/W2550 no-death; neutral and left-brake variants regressed to W2441 no-death. Treat W2440-W2644 forced shape changes as exhausted for this route class; next search should move before the W2352-W2440 second jump or use a different pre-W1753 route.

survival-v0.label: Robust Survival
survival-v0.summary: Prioritizes zero-death progression, safety override, fixed-threat handling, and stable clear routing.
survival-v0.playStyle: Single-AI clear baseline and parent route for all other strategy types.
survival-v0.riskPolicy: Safety, pits, body contact, and fixed-threat survival override speed, reward, and kill objectives.

speedrun-v0.label: Fast Advance
speedrun-v0.summary: Optimizes clear time after survival windows are stable.
speedrun-v0.riskPolicy: May skip optional kills and rewards, but cannot violate survival gates inherited from survival-v0.

combat-v0.label: Combat Clear
combat-v0.summary: Prioritizes enemy, turret, and fixed-target destruction while preserving clear stability.
combat-v0.playStyle: Threat-density reduction, kill-count farming, and safer human-facing companion play.
combat-v0.riskPolicy: Optional kills must not create dead loops or increase death probability beyond survival guardrails.

loot-v0.label: Loot Priority
loot-v0.summary: Prioritizes weapon and reward capture after survival windows are stable.
loot-v0.playStyle: Weapon optimization, reward viewing, and resource-rich companion play.
loot-v0.riskPolicy: Rewards are subordinate to survival and validated route continuity.

guard-v0.label: Companion Guard
guard-v0.summary: Prioritizes teammate safety, screen pacing, cover fire, and anti-screen-drag behavior.
guard-v0.playStyle: Human + AI and dual-AI cooperative play.
guard-v0.riskPolicy: Never steal dangerous rewards or drag the screen in a way that harms the teammate.

legacyNotes: Current training target is Contra Japan / contra-j-good.
Previous contra-us-good progress data was copied to training-progress.contra-us-good.legacy-broken-20260611.json because it was not valid JSON and must not be mixed with Japanese training.
All future accepted runs must use the Contra (J).nes hash in this ledger.
